title: A state boundary with an arrow-shaped void (the lock) alongside the governing `->` operator (the key). The void's exact shape matches the key exactly. Nothing else opens this state — no other operator, no other shape, no implicit path. This is Precept's from…on…-> triple made iconic: the state has exactly one interface, the event provides the key, and the transition fires only when they align.
conceptualBridge: The lock-key arrow means Precept's transition requires exact structural alignment because the state boundary has an arrow-shaped void that only admits the declared `->` operator — nothing else fits, and nothing else was ever declared.
decision: Superseded by 61-lock-key-v2 (avg 4.63) which uses identical concept with bolder proportions that survive 32px compression; keeping only the stronger variant preserves curated-set diversity.
